Okay. Alrighty. So one last point I want to make though with all this stuff. One of the biggest hangups I will see in people moving forward is this. People will go in and when we get to the funnel building phase, they will not launch because they believe that they need to have this amazing design, this incredible design. This, my funnel has got to look so freaking good. It's my brand. It's me. It's ha, right? This is why people buy for me. I will tell you that it's complete and utter garbage. It is a pile of stinking, rotting, putrescent filth, okay? That is so not true. Design has nothing to do with how well you sell. It doesn't. And I'm saying it doesn't. I'm going to say it doesn't again because I know a lot of you guys are throwing up the red flags. Well, not for me, Steven. Garbage. Okay. It's garbage. We went and we ran the top 100 grossing funnels inside of ClickFunnels. We looked at all top 100 of them and we started looking. We realized that 90% of them were the most but ugly funnels you ever seen in your entire life. And it was so shocking. We're like, what? We knew that design doesn't sue the selling. Design's not what does sales. But we started looking in and we started Oh my gosh. Like we were shocked at how bad the funnels were. And these guys are making tens of millions of dollars and they were the worst funnels you ever seen in your life. Okay, interesting. But the one thing that they all did have was a sales message. Okay, and offer and traffic. That's it. Those are the things that make sales happen. And anyway, when you second money, like after somebody's bought from you, after somebody's in the gate and you're starting to sell them, then branding can kind of start to play a little bit of a role. But what I don't want you guys to get distracted on is believing that design is what sells. It is a lie. Okay, it is a big fat lie. Design does not sell. I am not a designer, but I was the only funnel builder at ClickFunnels for a while besides Russell. We didn't have a designer. I was doing all the design. Okay, design. Okay, all the while these funnels dropping a million every three weeks. Bam, bam, bam, bam. And I'm building the next one, building the next one, building the next one. Russell's like, Hey, I got six more. Hey, finish one in three and 11 minutes. Hey, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa, I'm not a designer, but it's the principles behind sales and aligning your strategy with human psychology that makes the sale happen. It's got nothing to do with design. The reason I'm bringing that up with you guys right now is two things. Number one, we're going to go into funnel building in two weeks from now. I don't want you to exclude yourself from being sick. I can't do this because I'm not a designer. Okay, that's not true. Okay, number two, what I need you to understand is when we're making ebook covers, okay, there's a few areas where design helps. Okay, a sweet looking ebook cover can help a lot. This is one of those areas. So in the actual act of selling design has no role. But when you're making your product design covers, okay, when you're doing the actual covers like that, design can start to play a little bit more of a role. Okay, like Steven, I don't know how to design. Don't worry, I don't either. And I'm the only funnel builder in my personal business. Okay, you understand what I'm saying?
